title: Daily briefing: Funding calls plummet as NIH turns away from agency-directed science
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00897-y The US National Institutes of Health will now focus on ‘unsolicited’ research proposals driven by the interests of individual scientists. Plus, the oldest known recording of a whale’s song and how tiny electric vehicles can help to change the world.

ru.title: Ежедневный обзор: объемы финансирования резко снижаются, так как NIH отходит от направленной государством науки
ru.summary: Nature, опубликовано онлайн: 18 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00897-y Национальные институты здоровья США теперь сосредоточены на «несолицитированных» исследовательских предложениях, движимых интересами отдельных ученых. Кроме того, самая древняя известная запись песни кита и то, как крошечные электрические транспортные средства могут помочь изменить мир.

title: An enzyme inside the bacterial-cell membrane chops up viral DNA on entry
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00732-4 When a virus infects a bacterial cell, the viral genome is the first component to be fully injected into the cell, making it an ideal immune target. A bacterial enzyme anchored to the membrane intercepts and cleaves viral DNA during injection, providing a powerful form of antiviral defence.

ru.title: Фермент внутри бактериальной клеточной мембраны разрезает вирусную ДНК при попадании в клетку
ru.summary: Nature, опубликовано онлайн: 18 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00732-4 Когда вирус заражает бактериальную клетку, вирусный геном полностью впрыскивается в клетку первым, что делает его идеальной мишенью для иммунной системы. Бактериальный фермент, закрепленный на мембране, перехватывает и расщепляет вирусную ДНК во время инъекции, обеспечивая мощную форму противовирусной защиты.

title: [Comment] Should we keep pushing a high fluid intake in kidney stones?
summary: Urinary stone disease is common, associated with substantial morbidity, has a high recurrence rate, and affects quality of life.1,2 Consumption of large volumes of fluids is a foundational strategy for preventing stone formation,3 but adherence rates to this advice have been difficult to achieve in routine care.4 Patients frequently report practical, behavioural, and environmental barriers that hinder sustained behaviour change,5,6 and evidence regarding strategies to support long-term…

ru.title: [Комментарий] Должны ли мы продолжать настаивать на высоком потреблении жидкости при почечных камнях?
ru.summary: Мочекаменная болезнь распространена, связана со значительной заболеваемостью, имеет высокий уровень рецидивов и влияет на качество жизни.1,2 Потребление больших объемов жидкости является основной стратегией профилактики образования камней,3 однако соблюдение этих рекомендаций в обычной клинической практике было затруднено.4 Пациенты часто сообщают о практических, поведенческих и экологических барьерах, которые препятствуют устойчивому изменению поведения,5,6 и имеются доказательства, касающиеся стратегий поддержки долгосрочного…

title: [Comment] Surgical approach matters for long-term lung cancer outcomes
summary: For more than a decade, the medical community has sought to ascertain the oncological effect of perioperative management during cancer surgery.1 Despite encouraging preclinical study results showing that the choice of drugs used during or after surgery and the degree of invasiveness of the surgery could increase the risk of tumour recurrence, no clinical trials have succeeded in translating these results into clinical practice.2,3 This is particularly true for thoracic surgery, a specialty that…

ru.title: [Комментарий] Хирургический подход важен для долгосрочных результатов лечения рака легкого
ru.summary: Более десяти лет медицинское сообщество пытается установить онкологический эффект периоперационного ведения при онкологической хирургии.1 Несмотря на обнадеживающие доклинические исследования, показывающие, что выбор препаратов, используемых во время или после операции, и степень инвазивности хирургического вмешательства могут увеличить риск рецидива опухоли, ни одно клиническое исследование не смогло перевести эти результаты в клиническую практику.2,3 Это особенно верно для торакальной хирургии, специальности, которая…

title: [Perspectives] Claire Calderwood: integrated health screening for tuberculosis
summary: “Respiratory medicine is deeply connected to social and structural determinants of health in terms of the diseases that we try to prevent and we treat. I think that connection to people's lives and society more generally really appealed to me—it's tangible and as respiratory physicians we have an obligation to be advocates as well as clinicians”, says Claire Calderwood, Academic Clinical Lecturer in Respiratory Medicine at the National Heart and Lung Institute, Imperial College London, UK, and…

ru.title: [Перспективы] Клэр Калдервуд: комплексный скрининг здоровья при туберкулезе
ru.summary: "Респираторная медицина глубоко связана с социальными и структурными детерминантами здоровья с точки зрения болезней, которые мы пытаемся предотвратить и лечить. Я считаю, что эта связь с жизнью людей и обществом в целом действительно привлекла меня — это осязаемо, и как врачи-респираторологи мы обязаны быть защитниками, а также клиницистами", — говорит Клэр Калдервуд, доцент клинической респираторной медицины в Национальном институте сердца и легких, Имперский колледж Лондона, Великобритания, и…

title: [Correspondence] Closing the adolescent health financing gap: the Global Financing Facility
summary: Among today's most pressing development challenges is the matching of financing to health needs. One of the critically underfunded global priorities is adolescent health, with adolescents (aged 10–24 years) making up a quarter of the world's population but receiving only 2·4% of development aid for health in 2016–21.1

ru.title: [Переписка] Сокращение разрыва в финансировании здравоохранения подростков: Глобальный фонд финансирования
ru.summary: Одной из наиболее актуальных проблем развития является соответствие финансирования потребностям в здравоохранении. Одним из критически недофинансируемых глобальных приоритетов является здравоохранение подростков, при этом подростки (в возрасте 10–24 лет) составляют четверть населения мира, но получают только 2,4% помощи в развитии здравоохранения в 2016–2021 гг.

title: [Correspondence] The need for locally manufactured health commodities in Africa
summary: The 2021 Lancet Commission on diagnostics reported that nearly half of the world's population has limited access to basic diagnostic services.1 Less than 5% of diagnostics and less than 1% of vaccines used in Africa are produced locally.2 Heavy reliance on external supply is a strategic vulnerability, with consequences magnified during pandemics when global competition for medical countermeasures intensifies. Local manufacturing requires more than technical capacity: it demands policies that…

ru.title: [Переписка] Необходимость местного производства товаров здравоохранения в Африке
ru.summary: Доклад Комиссии Lancet 2021 года по диагностике установил, что почти половина населения мира имеет ограниченный доступ к основным диагностическим услугам. Менее 5% диагностических средств и менее 1% вакцин, используемых в Африке, производятся локально. Тяжелая зависимость от внешних поставок является стратегической уязвимостью, последствия которой усугубляются во время пандемий, когда глобальная конкуренция за медицинские средства интенсифицируется. Местное производство требует не только технических возможностей: оно требует политики, которая...

title: [Correspondence] Safeguarding against dengue fever risks in a more connected world
summary: Although malaria elimination has been reported in 14 countries, human health risk reduction against vector-borne diseases remains dire. With neither effective treatment nor widely available prophylaxis, and exacerbated by climate change, urbanisation, and population movements, dengue fever has emerged as a major planetary health threat.1 Currently, vector surveillance, chemical spraying, and biomedically modified vector experimentation are mainstream strategies for addressing mosquito-borne…

ru.title: [Корреспонденция] Защита от рисков лихорадки денге в более связанном мире

title: [Correspondence] Silence on genocide at the World Conference on Bioethics
summary: Between Nov 24 and Nov 27, 2025, the International Chair in Bioethics held its 17th World Conference on Bioethics, Medical Ethics, and Health Law in Ljubljana, Slovenia.1 Despite being hosted in a nation whose President has vocally condemned the “genocide in Gaza” at the UN, the conference stood in stark contrast to its setting.2 No scheduled sessions discussed the ongoing humanitarian catastrophe, representing a profound disconnect between the event's location and its content.

ru.title: [Корреспонденция] Молчание о геноциде на Всемирной конференции по биоэтике

title: [Correspondence] Respiratory virus research needs its own PPIE
summary: Patient perspectives are essential alongside clinical and economic evidence in health-care decision making, yet reporting of patient and participant involvement (PPI) in randomised controlled trials remains poor.1 After the COVID-19 pandemic, respiratory virus research in some countries has been deprioritised, with a sense that the public has “already lived through” the pandemic and there is little left to discuss.2,3

ru.title: [Корреспонденция] Исследования респираторных вирусов нуждаются в собственной PPIE

title: [Correspondence] PD-L1, treatment intensity, and outcomes in squamous NSCLC
summary: We read with interest the results of the HARMONi-6 trial by Zhiwei Chen and colleagues,1 which showed that ivonescimab plus chemotherapy improved progression-free survival versus tislelizumab plus chemotherapy in advanced squamous non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC). However, PD-L1-based subgroup results reveal a critical stratification signal. In patients with PD-L1 tumour proportion score (TPS) of under 50%, dual blockade achieved marked benefit (hazard ratio [HR] 0·63; 95% CI 0·41–0·98),…

ru.title: [Корреспонденция] PD-L1, интенсивность лечения и исходы при плоскоклеточном НМРЛ

title: [Articles] Angiography-derived fractional flow reserve versus coronary angiography to guide coronary artery bypass grafting in patients undergoing surgical valve procedures with concomitant coronary artery disease in China (FAVOR IV-QVAS): a multicentre, triple-blind, randomised trial
summary: Among patients undergoing valve surgery with concomitant coronary artery disease, physiologically guided CABG using angiography-derived FFR reduced the incidence of the composite perioperative outcome compared with anatomically guided CABG. These findings support a selective approach to surgical coronary revascularisation guided by physiological assessment in patients undergoing valve procedures.

ru.title: [Статьи] Фракционный коронарный резерв, полученный при ангиографии, в сравнении с коронарной ангиографией для ведения пациентов, подвергающихся хирургической коррекции клапанных пороков с сопутствующим поражением коронарных артерий в Китае (FAVOR IV-QVAS): многоцентровое, тройное слепое, рандомизированное исследование

title: [Articles] Survival outcome of VATS compared with open lobectomy for lung cancer: an individual patient data meta-analysis of randomised trials
summary: This meta-analysis provides evidence that surgical access by VATS lobectomy improved overall survival compared with open surgery without any compromise to disease-free survival. These results underscore the importance of prioritising VATS when technically feasible as the access of choice for surgical resection of early-stage non-small-cell lung cancer.

ru.title: [Статьи] Выживаемость при видеоассистированной торакальной хирургии в сравнении с открытой лобэктомией при раке легкого: метаанализ индивидуальных данных пациентов из рандомизированных исследований

title: ‘Unaffordable’ visa price hike threatens Australia’s researcher pipeline
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00822-3 Doubling of the cost of a key visa used by graduates to stay in the country between jobs has sparked critique and consternation.

ru.title: «Недоступный» рост цен на визы угрожает трубопроводу исследователей Австралии
ru.summary: Nature, опубликовано в интернете: 20 марта 2026 г.; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00822-3 Удвоение стоимости ключевой визы, используемой выпускниками для остановки в стране между работами, вызвало критику и возмущение.

title: The mid-career reset: how to be strategic about your research direction
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-04077-2 A decade after completing a PhD, your research can begin to lose direction. So consider each opportunity carefully, says Mohamed Ezat.

ru.title: Переосмысление карьеры в середине: как стратегически подойти к направлению своего исследования
ru.summary: Nature, опубликовано в интернете: 20 марта 2026 г.; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-04077-2 Десять лет спустя после защиты докторской диссертации ваше исследование может начать терять направление. Поэтому тщательно рассмотрите каждую возможность, говорит Мохаммед Езат.

title: Paul R. Ehrlich obituary: pioneering ecologist who caused controversy by predicting a ‘population bomb’
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00939-5 The environmentalist founded the field of co-evolution, and warned that rapid human expansion would cause biodiversity collapse and famine.

ru.title: Некролог Пола Р. Эрлиха: пионер экологии, вызвавший противоречие своим предсказанием «демографической бомбы»

title: Adaptive evolution of gene regulatory networks in mammalian neocortex
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10226-y The excitatory neuron diversity and specialized connectivity of complex, multilayered mammalian neocortex are driven by mammalian-specific cis-regulatory elements bound by ZBTB18, deletion of which disrupts gene expression and results in projection patterns resembling those of non-mammalian brains.

ru.title: Адаптивная эволюция сетей генной регуляции в неокоре млекопитающих

title: Local agricultural transition, crisis and migration in the Southern Andes
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10233-z In the Uspallata Valley, agriculture was adopted by local populations, as evidenced by genetic continuity from earlier hunter-gatherers to farmers; maize-dependent groups from the same regional population later experienced stress and demographic decline and likely used social organization and migration as resilience strategies.

ru.title: Местный сельскохозяйственный переход, кризис и миграция в Южных Андах

title: Contrasting thermophilization among forests, grasslands and alpine summits
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09622-7 Analyses of large-scale, multitaxa and long-term thermophilization patterns in forests, grasslands and alpine summits across Europe provide insight into shifts in community composition among different ecosystems in a warming world.

ru.title: Контрастные закономерности термофилизации в лесах, травяных сообществах и на альпийских вершинах

title: Proteasome-guided haem signalling axis contributes to T cell exhaustion
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10250-y Exhausted CD8+ T cells increase proteasome activity due to the accumulation of depolarized mitochondria, which drives the selective degradation of mitochondrial proteins and releases of regulatory haem through haemoprotein breakdown.

ru.title: Протеасома-опосредованный гемовый сигнальный путь способствует истощению T-клеток

title: Integrated photonic neural network with on-chip backpropagation training
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10262-8 An integrated photonic deep neural network was trained end-to-end with on-chip gradient-descent backpropagation, and all linear and nonlinear computations were performed on a single photonic chip, resulting in a reliable performance despite on-chip errors and variations.

ru.title: Интегрированная фотонная нейронная сеть с обучением обратного распространения на кристалле

title: The E3 ubiquitin ligase mechanism specifying targeted microRNA degradation
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10232-0 Target-directed microRNA degradation is driven by the atypical ZSWIM8–CUL3 E3 ubiquitin ligase that uses a two-RNA-factor authentication mechanism to specifically recognize AGO–miRNA–trigger RNA complexes and polyubiquitylate AGO.

ru.title: Механизм E3 убиквитин-лигазы, определяющий целевую деградацию микроРНК

title: Adventitious carbon breaks symmetry in oxide contact electrification
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10088-w By measuring charge exchange in a sphere/plate pair composed of identical amorphous silicon dioxide and controlling charging polarity using baking or plasma treatment, adventitious carbon is shown to break symmetry in oxide contact electrification.

ru.title: Побочный углерод нарушает симметрию в контактной электризации оксидов

title: A strong constraint on radiative forcing of well-mixed greenhouse gases
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10289-x Comparing line-by-line transfer simulations using the radiation code GRTcode with regressions against satellite-observed ongoing longwave radiation shows that instantaneous longwave radiative forcing from well-mixed greenhouse gases has increased by 3.69 ± 0.07 W m−2 since 1850.

ru.title: Строгое ограничение радиационного воздействия хорошо перемешанных парниковых газов

title: Genome editing that avoids immune detection to integrate large DNA sequences
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00730-6 A genome-editing approach called INSTALL combines single-stranded DNA molecules that evade immune detection with a short double-stranded region that allows recombinase enzymes to insert a DNA sequence into mammalian genomes. INSTALL enables the insertion of large DNA sequences into the genomes of human and mouse cells without causing toxic immune responses.

ru.title: Редактирование генома, избегающее иммунного обнаружения для интеграции больших последовательностей ДНК

title: Mystery of how plants make a family of medicinal molecules has been solved
summary: Nature, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00790-8 The cinchona alkaloids are a family of plant-derived compounds that include quinine, an antimalarial drug also used as the bittering ingredient in tonic water. The biosynthetic pathway in Cinchona plants that produces the scaffold for these important molecules has been mapped.

ru.title: Разгадана загадка того, как растения производят семейство лекарственных молекул
ru.summary: Nature, опубликовано в интернете: 18 марта 2026 г.; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00790-8 Алкалоиды хинхоны — это семейство растительных соединений, включающих хинин, противомалярийный препарат, также используемый в качестве горьковатого ингредиента в тоническом напитке. Биосинтетический путь в растениях Cinchona, который производит основу для этих важных молекул, был нанесен на карту.

title: [Editorial] Making treatment for obesity more equitable
summary: 2026 may prove a pivotal year for obesity. GLP-1 receptor agonists have revolutionised obesity management over the past 10 years. Mounting evidence has established their benefits across many cardiometabolic and obesity-related conditions and the global weight-loss medications market is predicted to reach US$150 billion by 2035. More than a billion people live with obesity, with the burden rising rapidly in low-income and middle-income countries. High costs, limited production capacity, and…

ru.title: [Редакционная статья] Обеспечение справедливости в лечении ожирения

title: [Comment] Physiologically guided CABG in valve surgery
summary: Physiological evaluation using fractional flow reserve (FFR) provides more accurate assessment of the haemodynamic significance of coronary artery stenosis than coronary angiography alone. FFR-guided percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) results in better short-term and long-term outcomes than PCI guided by coronary angiography or medical therapy alone.1,2 Angiography-derived FFR is a way to estimate FFR from standard coronary angiography images using computational fluid dynamics or related…

title: [Comment] Offline: Intelligence does not prevent stupidity
summary: In The Wizard of the Kremlin (2022), Giuliano da Empoli imagines a former political advisor to Vladimir Putin recounting the rise of his new tsar from spook to dictator. (Before becoming a writer, da Empoli was himself a political advisor—to Italy's Prime Minister Matteo Renzi.) His principal character, Vadim Baranov, contrasts the West, where “your society is based on the principle that nothing is greater than money”, with Russia, where the people sought “someone at the top who could guarantee…

title: [Correspondence] On the computability of biological machines
summary: In the book Natural Theology (1802), the English philosopher and theologian William Paley argued that the complexity of an organism, similar to that of a watch, implied the handiwork of a designer.1 His reasoning rested on the intuition that intricate organisation could not arise from compact generative principles.

ru.title: [Корреспонденция] О вычислимости биологических систем

title: Remote monitoring of heart failure exacerbations using a smartwatch
summary: Nature Medicine, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04247-3 A deep learning model using smartwatch data was shown to predict peak oxygen uptake and unplanned healthcare events in the TRUE-HF prospective cohort of patients with heart failure, as well as unplanned healthcare utilization in patients with heart failure in the All of Us Research Program.

ru.title: Удаленный мониторинг обострений сердечной недостаточности с помощью умных часов
ru.summary: Nature Medicine, опубликовано онлайн: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04247-3 Модель глубокого обучения, использующая данные со смартфона, показала способность предсказывать максимальное потребление кислорода и незапланированные события в области здравоохранения в проспективной когорте TRUE-HF пациентов с сердечной недостаточностью, а также незапланированное использование медицинских услуг у пациентов с сердечной недостаточностью в программе All of Us Research Program.

title: Real-world clinical utility of tumor whole-genome sequencing in solid cancers
summary: Nature Medicine, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04280-2 In a real-world cohort of 888 patients with solid cancers receiving whole-genome sequencing profiling, actionable biomarkers were identified in 73% of patients, and targeted therapies were associated with clinical benefit in patients without prior systemic therapy.

ru.title: Клиническая полезность полногеномного секвенирования опухолей при солидных раках в реальной практике
ru.summary: Nature Medicine, опубликовано онлайн: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04280-2 В реальной когорте из 888 пациентов с солидными раками, прошедших профилирование путем полногеномного секвенирования, определяемые биомаркеры были выявлены у 73% пациентов, и таргетная терапия была связана с клиническим эффектом у пациентов без предшествующей системной терапии.

title: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists for major cardiovascular and kidney outcomes in type 1 diabetes
summary: Nature Medicine, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04274-0 A trial emulation study using electronic health record data from 174,678 patients with type 1 diabetes revealed that GLP-1RA initiation is associated with reduced risk of major cardiovascular events and end-stage kidney disease.

ru.title: Агонисты рецепторов глюкагоноподобного пептида-1 для основных сердечно-сосудистых и почечных исходов при сахарном диабете 1 типа
ru.summary: Nature Medicine, опубликовано онлайн: 19 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41591-026-04274-0 Исследование-эмуляция, использующее данные электронных медицинских записей от 174 678 пациентов с сахарным диабетом 1 типа, показало, что инициирование GLP-1RA связано со снижением риска основных сердечно-сосудистых событий и терминальной стадии хронической болезни почек.

title: Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life
summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03064-w This Review discusses the promises and pitfalls of biological AI algorithms and presents a vision for generalist biological artificial intelligence, in which models can perform diverse tasks across biological domains.

ru.title: Универсальный биологический искусственный интеллект при моделировании языка жизни

Переформатированное микроокружение опухоли синергически взаимодействует с блокадой иммунной контрольной точки PD-L1, восстанавливая активность CD8+ Т-клеток и обеспечивая длительную регрессию опухоли в моделях на мышах.

title: Risk of Cardiorespiratory Events Increases After Severe RSV Infection
summary: Severe respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection was associated with a higher risk of cardiorespiratory events in the 2 weeks following hospitalization—and in some cases longer—according to research published in JAMA Network Open. The findings add to the growing body of evidence surrounding long-term consequences of severe RSV infection, similar to other respiratory viruses like SARS-CoV-2.

ru.title: Риск кардиореспираторных событий повышается после тяжелой инфекции РСВ
ru.summary: Тяжелая инфекция респираторно-синцитиального вируса (РСВ) была связана с повышенным риском кардиореспираторных событий в течение 2 недель после госпитализации — и в некоторых случаях дольше — согласно исследованию, опубликованному в JAMA Network Open. Полученные результаты дополняют растущий объем доказательств долгосрочных последствий тяжелой инфекции РСВ, аналогично другим респираторным вирусам, таким как SARS-CoV-2.

title: Personalized Hemodynamic Resuscitation in Early Septic Shock—Reply
summary: In Reply We thank Dr Bosch for his thoughtful comments regarding the ANDROMEDA-SHOCK-2 trial and welcome the opportunity to clarify the important points raised. Bosch argues that eFigure 5 may demonstrate heterogeneity of treatment effect across study sites (the intervention was beneficial at some centers while possibly harmful at others) and that this would raise concerns about the validity and implementation of the protocol. Nevertheless, we have another view about this interpretation.

ru.title: Персонализированная гемодинамическая реанимация при ранней септическом шоке — Ответ
ru.summary: В ответ Мы благодарны доктору Бошу за его вдумчивые замечания относительно исследования ANDROMEDA-SHOCK-2 и приветствуем возможность уточнить важные поднятые вопросы. Бош утверждает, что эфигура 5 может демонстрировать гетерогенность эффекта лечения в разных центрах исследования (вмешательство было полезно в некоторых центрах и, возможно, вредно в других) и что это вызывает опасения по поводу обоснованности и имплементации протокола. Тем не менее, мы придерживаемся иной точки зрения на эту интерпретацию.

title: Buprenorphine in the ED—Balancing Access, Safety, and Autonomy
summary: In the context of an ongoing public health emergency related to drug toxicity deaths, emergency department (ED) visits are undeniably important opportunities to identify individuals with high-risk opioid use and to engage them in care. Emergency departments are available every hour of the day, every day of the year in many communities and provide low-barrier touchpoints for individuals with high-risk opioid use, many of whom have concurrent social vulnerabilities. Increasing access to…

ru.title: Бупренорфин в отделении неотложной помощи — балансирование между доступностью, безопасностью и автономией
ru.summary: В контексте продолжающейся кризиса общественного здоровья, связанного со смертностью от токсичности наркотиков, посещения отделения неотложной помощи (ОНП) являются неоспоримо важными возможностями для выявления лиц с высокорисковым употреблением опиоидов и привлечения их к получению медицинской помощи. Отделения неотложной помощи доступны круглосуточно, семь дней в неделю во многих сообществах и обеспечивают низкопороговые точки контакта для лиц с высокорисковым употреблением опиоидов, многие из которых имеют сопутствующие социальные уязвимости. Увеличение доступа к...

title: Treatment for New-Onset AF After CABG—Reply
summary: In Reply We thank Dr Xu and colleagues for their thoughtful remarks on our study, providing us an opportunity to clarify some aspects of our analyses. First, we agree that new-onset AF after CABG is a distinct entity, not to be confused with AF not associated with surgery. Several registry studies have reported that postoperative AF is associated with a markedly lower risk of thromboembolic events and death than nonvalvular AF occurring outside the surgical setting. Our study adds to the…

ru.title: Лечение новой фибрилляции предсердий после операции коронарного шунтирования — ответ
ru.summary: В ответ Мы благодарим д-ра Сюя и коллег за их вдумчивые замечания относительно нашего исследования, которые дают нам возможность уточнить некоторые аспекты наших анализов. Во-первых, мы согласны, что новая фибрилляция предсердий после операции коронарного шунтирования является отдельным явлением и не должна путаться с фибрилляцией предсердий, не связанной с хирургией. Несколько реестровых исследований сообщили, что послеоперационная фибрилляция предсердий связана со значительно более низким риском тромбоэмболических событий и смерти по сравнению с неклапанной фибрилляцией предсердий, возникающей вне хирургических условий. Наше исследование дополняет...

title: A complete set of canonical nucleobases in the carbonaceous asteroid (162173) Ryugu
summary: Nature Astronomy, Published online: 16 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41550-026-02791-z Samples returned from the asteroid Ryugu contain all five canonical nucleobases (A, G, C, T and U). Their presence in Ryugu and Bennu supports the hypothesis that carbonaceous asteroids contributed to the prebiotic chemical inventory of early Earth.

ru.title: Полный набор канонических нуклеобаз в углистом астероиде (162173) Рюгу
ru.summary: Nature Astronomy, опубликовано онлайн: 16 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41550-026-02791-z Образцы, возвращенные с астероида Рюгу, содержат все пять канонических нуклеобаз (A, G, C, T и U). Их присутствие на Рюгу и Бенну подтверждает гипотезу о том, что углистые астероиды способствовали додиотическому химическому составу ранней Земли.

title: Enrichment by the first stars in a relic dwarf galaxy
summary: Nature Astronomy, Published online: 16 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41550-026-02802-z A star in a primordial dwarf galaxy has preserved the elements produced by the first generation of stars. The star lacks heavy elements but exhibits an extreme amount of carbon, suggesting that low-energy explosions can seed the initial chemistry of early galaxies.

ru.title: Обогащение первыми звездами в релликовой карликовой галактике
ru.summary: Nature Astronomy, опубликовано онлайн: 16 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41550-026-02802-z Звезда в первобытной карликовой галактике сохранила элементы, производимые первым поколением звезд. Звезда лишена тяжелых элементов, но демонстрирует экстремальное количество углерода, что предполагает, что низкоэнергетические взрывы могут инициировать первичную химию ранних галактик.

title: Cardiovascular Disease Risk Climbs Earlier for Men
summary: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) risk increases for men years before it does for women, a prospective study published in the Journal of the American Heart Association found. The study aimed to investigate whether the well-established earlier onset of coronary heart disease in men persists in contemporary populations and whether age at onset differs between the sexes for other types of cardiovascular disease.

ru.title: Риск сердечно-сосудистых заболеваний возрастает раньше у мужчин
ru.summary: Риск сердечно-сосудистых заболеваний (ССЗ) увеличивается у мужчин на годы раньше, чем у женщин, согласно перспективному исследованию, опубликованному в журнале Journal of the American Heart Association. Исследование было направлено на установление того, сохраняется ли хорошо документированное более раннее начало ишемической болезни сердца у мужчин в современных популяциях и различается ли возраст начала между полами для других типов сердечно-сосудистых заболеваний.

title: Sunshine and Skyshine
summary: The importance of sunshine for the welfare of plants has long been recognized. It has also been suspected, to express it mildly, that the vegetative functions of animal kind in general and man in particular derive some sort of benefit from the same agency. Fresh air and sunshine have formed the basis of innumerable prescriptions to those who seek to regain good health or to retain it. Only recently, however, has the significance of the invisible radiations begun to be appreciated. As Hess has…

title: Poetry and Intimacy in the Patient-Physician Relationship
summary: Love and intimacy informing poetry is as old as poetry itself. Perhaps poetry’s abiding association with the erotic contributes to medicine’s unease with it, where professional boundaries are essential to safeguarding patients who make themselves vulnerable both by disclosing personal information and the exposure of the physical examination. Yet poetry can be invaluable to medicine as a means for articulating and exploring this charged and omnipresent aspect of clinical care, as few if any…

title: Personalized Hemodynamic Resuscitation in Early Septic Shock
summary: To the Editor The ANDROMEDA-SHOCK-2 randomized clinical trial reported that patients randomized to a multicomponent septic shock resuscitation strategy experienced benefit compared with those who were randomized to usual care, based on a win ratio analysis of mortality, duration of vital support, and length of stay. There was no evidence that the benefit of the intervention differed based on subgroups stratified by participant age, multiple measures of severity of illness, and septic shock…

ru.title: Персонализированная гемодинамическая реанимация при ранних стадиях септического шока

title: Treatment for New-Onset AF After CABG
summary: To the Editor For those of us caring for patients after coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG), postoperative atrial fibrillation (AF) is common, but not all AF is the same. Dr Herrmann and colleagues used continuous implantable monitoring to show that postoperative AF is frequent yet often brief and low in burden. Their findings raise a practical question cardiac surgeons face daily: Should every postoperative episode be treated as chronic AF when considering anticoagulation?

ru.title: Лечение вновь развившейся фибрилляции предсердий после аортокоронарного шунтирования

title: Trends and Disparities of US Adult Obesity Prevalence
summary: This study uses measured body mass index (BMI) data from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey and bias-corrected BMI values calculated from self-reported height and weight data from the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System and Gallup Daily Survey to estimate US obesity prevalence from 1990 to 2022 and predict trends through 2035 by race and ethnicity, state, sex, and age.

ru.title: Тенденции и различия в распространённости ожирения у взрослого населения США

title: Printable Newtonian fluid photocatalysts for scale-up solar CO 2 conversion
summary: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70819-z The Newtonian fluid photocatalyst composed of imidazole framework and light-excitable liquid chain can be firmly attached to different types of carriers through printing for easier photocatalytic scaling up and more stable CO2 photoreduction.

ru.title: Печатные ньютоновские жидкофотокатализаторы для масштабированного солнечного преобразования CO2
ru.summary: Nature Communications, опубликовано в сети: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70819-z Ньютоновский жидкий фотокатализатор, состоящий из имидазольного каркаса и световозбуждаемой жидкой цепи, может быть прочно прикреплен к различным типам носителей путем печати для более легкого масштабирования фотокатализа и более стабильной фотовосстановления CO2.

title: A disease-agnostic approach to ensemble learning for infectious disease forecasting
summary: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70937-8 Multi-model ensembles of infectious disease forecasts tend to perform better than individual forecasts, but assigning appropriate weights to component models in the ensemble is challenging. Here, the authors present epiFFORMA, a method for automatic component weighting that does not require historical data.

ru.title: Независимый от заболевания подход к ансамблевому обучению для прогнозирования инфекционных заболеваний
ru.summary: Nature Communications, опубликовано в сети: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70937-8 Многомодельные ансамбли прогнозов инфекционных заболеваний обычно показывают лучшие результаты, чем отдельные прогнозы, но назначение надлежащих весов компонентным моделям в ансамбле является сложной задачей. Здесь авторы представляют epiFFORMA, метод автоматического взвешивания компонентов, который не требует исторических данных.

title: One-step fabrication of superhydrophobic fabrics with stable mechanical performance in harsh conditions
summary: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70857-7 Developing durable, water-repellent textiles without using harmful fluorinated chemicals is challenging. Here, authors use a one-step molecular assembly to covalently bond superhydrophobic silica shells directly onto fibers, resulting in high wear resistance and breathability.

ru.title: Одностадийное производство супергидрофобных тканей с устойчивым механическим поведением в жестких условиях
ru.summary: Nature Communications, опубликовано онлайн: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70857-7 Разработка прочных водоотталкивающих текстильных материалов без использования вредных фторированных химических веществ представляет значительные трудности. Авторы используют одностадийную молекулярную сборку для ковалентного присоединения супергидрофобных кремнеземных оболочек непосредственно к волокнам, что обеспечивает высокую износостойкость и воздухопроницаемость.

title: Parvalbumin-positive neurons in the medial septum participate in the formation of hippocampal-dependent spatial memory
summary: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70268-8 This study shows that medial septal parvalbumin neurons causally control hippocampal place cell dynamics during memory encoding, thereby supporting object-place recognition and rescuing sleep deprivation-induced memory deficits.

ru.title: Парвальбумин-позитивные нейроны медиального перегородки участвуют в формировании зависящей от гиппокампа пространственной памяти
ru.summary: Nature Communications, опубликовано онлайн: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70268-8 Это исследование показывает, что парвальбумин-позитивные нейроны медиального перегородки причинно контролируют динамику гиппокампальных локус-нейронов во время кодирования памяти, поддерживая распознавание объект-место и восстанавливая дефициты памяти, вызванные депривацией сна.

title: Copper single-atom nanozyme with intelligent capture and photo-enhanced activity for controlling plant bacterial diseases
summary: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70930-1 Nanozymes are promising for controlling plant bacterial diseases, but conventional nanozymes suffer from low bacterial affinity, inefficient enzyme-like activity, and thus poor antibacterial efficacy. Here, the authors report a copper single-atom nanozyme that outperforms commercial thiodiazole copper in controlling plant bacterial diseases via intelligent capture and photo-enhanced activity.

ru.title: Медь-содержащий одноатомный нанозим с умным захватом и фото-усиленной активностью для контроля бактериальных болезней растений
ru.summary: Nature Communications, опубликовано онлайн: 20 марта 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70930-1 Нанозимы являются перспективными для контроля бактериальных болезней растений, однако традиционные нанозимы страдают от низкого сродства к бактериям, неэффективной ферментативной активности и, как следствие, низкой антибактериальной эффективности. Авторы сообщают о медь-содержащем одноатомном нанозиме, который превосходит коммерческие препараты на основе медь-тиодиазола в контроле бактериальных болезней растений благодаря умному захвату и фото-усиленной активности.

title: Scientists turn CO2 into fuel using breakthrough single-atom catalyst
summary: Researchers have created a cutting-edge catalyst that turns CO2 into methanol more efficiently than ever before. Instead of using clumps of metal atoms, they engineered a system where each single indium atom actively drives the reaction. This dramatically reduces energy needs while making the process easier to study and optimize. The result could accelerate the shift toward cleaner fuels and sustainable chemical production.

ru.title: Ученые превращают CO2 в топливо, используя революционный однoатомный катализатор
ru.summary: Исследователи создали передовой катализатор, который превращает CO2 в метанол более эффективно, чем когда-либо прежде. Вместо использования скоплений атомов металла они разработали систему, в которой каждый отдельный атом индия активно ускоряет реакцию. Это значительно снижает энергетические затраты и упрощает изучение и оптимизацию процесса. Результат может ускорить переход к более чистому топливу и устойчивому химическому производству.

title: Astronomers discover nearby galaxy was shattered by cosmic crash
summary: A nearby galaxy is behaving strangely—and now scientists know why. The Small Magellanic Cloud’s stars move in chaotic patterns because it slammed into its larger neighbor millions of years ago. That collision disrupted its structure and even created the illusion that its gas was rotating. The discovery means this once “textbook” galaxy may not be as typical as astronomers believed.

ru.title: Астрономы обнаружили, что соседняя галактика была разрушена космическим столкновением
ru.summary: Соседняя галактика ведет себя странно — и теперь ученые знают почему. Звезды Малого Магелланова Облака движутся в хаотичных паттернах, потому что миллионы лет назад оно столкнулось со своим более крупным соседом. Это столкновение нарушило его структуру и даже создало иллюзию того, что его газ вращается. Открытие означает, что эта когда-то «учебная» галактика может быть не такой типичной, как считали астрономы.

title: MIT scientists finally see hidden quantum “jiggling” inside superconductors
summary: MIT physicists have built a powerful new microscope that uses terahertz light to uncover hidden quantum motions inside superconductors. By compressing this normally unwieldy light into a tiny region, they were able to observe electrons moving together in a frictionless, wave-like state for the first time. This discovery opens a new window into how superconductors really work. It could also help drive future breakthroughs in high-speed wireless communication.

ru.title: Учёные MIT наконец увидели скрытое квантовое «дрожание» внутри сверхпроводников
ru.summary: Физики MIT создали мощный новый микроскоп, который использует терагерцевый свет для выявления скрытых квантовых движений внутри сверхпроводников. Сжимая обычно громоздкий свет в крошечную область, им удалось впервые наблюдать электроны, движущиеся вместе в безтрёточном волнообразном состоянии. Это открытие открывает новое окно в понимание того, как на самом деле работают сверхпроводники. Это также может помочь привести к будущим прорывам в высокоскоростной беспроводной связи.

title: Archaeological site in Chile upends theory of how humans populated the Americas … again
summary: Discovery at Monte Verde puts north-to-south expansion theory back at centre of heated debate on continent’s human history A groundbreaking new study may have once again upended our understanding of human prehistory in the Americas. For years, the predominant theory of how humans arrived in the western hemisphere centred around the Clovis culture, which crossed the Beringia land bridge from Asia between 13,400 and 12,800 years ago, and spread south. Continue reading...

ru.summary: Открытие в Монте-Верде снова ставит в центр острых дебатов теорию расширения с севера на юг о истории человечества континента. Новаторское исследование может снова изменить наше понимание доисторического человечества в Америке. Годами господствующей теорией о том, как человек прибыл в западное полушарие, была теория, сосредоточенная на культуре Кловис, которая пересекла сухопутный мост Беринджии из Азии между 13 400 и 12 800 лет назад и распространилась на юг.

title: Kent meningitis outbreak may have peaked as UKHSA reports slowdown in cases
summary: Experts continue to analyse strain of MenB to understand whether it has become more likely to spread or cause disease The Kent meningitis outbreak may have reached its peak after only two new cases were reported by officials on Friday. The UK Health and Security Agency said that as of 12.30pm on Thursday, there were 18 confirmed and 11 probable cases of meningitis linked to the Kent outbreak , taking the total number of people with the disease to 29. Of the confirmed cases, 13 were meningitis B…

ru.title: Вспышка менингита в Кенте, похоже, достигла пика, так как UKHSA сообщает о замедлении случаев
ru.summary: Эксперты продолжают анализировать штамм MenB, чтобы понять, стал ли он более склонен к распространению или вызыванию заболевания. Вспышка менингита в Кенте, возможно, достигла своего пика, так как официально было зарегистрировано только два новых случая в пятницу. Агентство здравоохранения и безопасности Великобритании сообщило, что по состоянию на 12:30 в четверг было 18 подтвёрждённых и 11 вероятных случаев менингита, связанных со вспышкой в Кенте, в результате чего общее число людей с этим заболеванием составило 29. Из подтвёрждённых случаев 13 были менингитом B.

title: Nasa returns moon rocket to pad and targets 1 April launch
summary: After series of delays, US space agency hopes to carry out first crewed flyby of the moon in more than half a century Nasa has begun returning its towering SLS rocket and Orion spacecraft to its Florida launch pad before a planned flyby of the moon, after completing necessary repairs. Artemis engineers began the manoeuvre, which can take up to 12 hours, at 8pm local time. The US space agency will then begin the final preparations before its next launch window opens on 1 April. ru.title: NASA возвращает лунную ракету на стартовую площадку и нацелена на запуск 1 апреля
ru.summary: После серии задержек американское космическое агентство надеется осуществить первый пилотируемый облёт луны более чем за полвека. NASA начало возвращение своей огромной ракеты SLS и космического корабля Orion на его стартовую площадку во Флориде после завершения необходимого ремонта. Инженеры Artemis начали манёвр, который может занять до 12 часов, в 20:00 по местному времени. Американское космическое агентство затем начнёт окончательные приготовления перед тем, как его следующее окно запуска откроется 1 апреля.

title: The Guardian view on meningitis in Kent: we must not take public health systems for granted | Editorial
summary: This frightening outbreak is not yet over, and serves as a reminder of why plans to manage infectious diseases exist The public health measures taken in response to this month’s meningitis outbreak in Kent so far appear to be working . Two young people have tragically died – one a sixth-former in Faversham, the other a student at the University of Kent. In the Canterbury area, where cases have been identified at four schools and two universities, thousands of lives have been disrupted and many…

ru.summary: Эта страшная вспышка еще не закончена и служит напоминанием о том, почему существуют планы по управлению инфекционными заболеваниями. Меры общественного здравоохранения, принятые в ответ на вспышку менингита в Кенте в этом месяце, похоже, работают. Трагически погибли два молодых человека – один старшеклассник в Фавершеме, другой студент Кентского университета. В районе Кентербери, где случаи выявлены в четырёх школах и двух университетах, жизни тысяч людей были нарушены и многие.

summary: Eating large amounts of ultra-processed foods like chips, frozen meals, sugary drinks, and packaged snacks may significantly raise the risk of serious heart problems. In a major U.S. study, people consuming around nine servings per day had a 67% higher risk of heart attacks, strokes, or death from heart disease compared to those eating about one serving. The risk didn’t just jump at high levels either. Each additional daily serving increased the likelihood of these events by more than 5%, even…

В крупном американском исследовании люди, потребляющие около девяти порций в день, имели на 67% выше риск инфарктов, инсультов или смерти от сердечного заболевания по сравнению с теми, кто потребляет примерно одну порцию. Риск не возникает резко только при высоких уровнях. Каждая дополнительная ежедневная порция увеличивала вероятность этих событий более чем на 5%, даже...

title: Gum disease bacterium linked to breast cancer growth and spread
summary: A common oral bacterium tied to gum disease may help spark and fuel breast cancer, according to new research. Scientists discovered it can travel through the bloodstream to breast tissue, where it causes DNA damage and speeds tumor growth and spread. It also appears to make cancer cells more aggressive and resistant to therapy. The effect is even stronger in people with BRCA1 mutations, raising new questions about the role of oral health in cancer risk.

ru.title: Бактерия, вызывающая заболевание десен, связана с ростом и распространением рака молочной железы
ru.summary: Обычная оральная бактерия, связанная с заболеванием десен, может способствовать возникновению и развитию рака молочной железы, согласно новому исследованию. Ученые обнаружили, что она может распространяться через кровоток в ткань молочной железы, где вызывает повреждение ДНК и ускоряет рост и распространение опухоли. Она также, похоже, делает раковые клетки более агрессивными и устойчивыми к терапии. Эффект еще более выражен у людей с мутациями BRCA1, что поднимает новые вопросы о роли здоровья полости рта в риске развития рака.

title: Scientists solve 12,800-year-old climate mystery hidden in Greenland ice
summary: A mysterious spike of platinum buried deep in Greenland’s ice has long fueled theories of a catastrophic comet or asteroid strike 12,800 years ago—possibly triggering a sudden return to icy conditions known as the Younger Dryas. But new research points to a far less dramatic, yet still powerful culprit: volcanic eruptions. Scientists found the platinum signal doesn’t match space debris and actually appeared decades after the cooling began, ruling out an impact as the trigger.

title: This virus therapy supercharges the immune system against brain cancer
summary: Scientists have found a way to make one of the most aggressive brain tumors vulnerable to the immune system. A single injection of a modified virus can invade glioblastoma, kill cancer cells, and summon immune fighters deep into the tumor. These immune cells persist and attack, which was linked to longer survival in patients.

title: This common vaccine cuts heart risk nearly in half in new study
summary: A shingles vaccine might double as a powerful heart protector. In people already at high risk, it cut major cardiac events by 46% and deaths by an impressive 66% within a year. Scientists think preventing shingles may also stop clot-related complications that can lead to heart attacks and strokes. The effect is so strong, it rivals the benefits of quitting smoking.

title: Huge study finds no evidence cannabis helps anxiety, depression, or PTSD
summary: The largest review of medicinal cannabis to date found it doesn’t effectively treat anxiety, depression, or PTSD—despite millions using it for those reasons. Researchers warn it could even make mental health worse, raising risks like psychosis and addiction while delaying proven treatments. Some limited benefits were seen for conditions like insomnia and autism, but the evidence is weak. The findings are fueling calls for stricter oversight as cannabis use continues to rise.

summary: Malaria parasites contain tiny spinning crystals that have puzzled scientists for years. New research reveals they’re powered by a rocket-like reaction that breaks down hydrogen peroxide, releasing energy. This motion may help the parasite detoxify harmful chemicals and manage iron more efficiently. The discovery could lead to new drugs and spark innovations in microscopic robotics.

title: Найден маленький рибозим-полимераза, способный реплицировать сам себя
summary: Согласно теории РНК-мира, на ранних этапах абиогенеза важную роль играли рибозимы-полимеразы — молекулы РНК, способные размножать молекулы РНК, в том числе самих себя. До сих пор ученым удавалось получить только крупные рибозимы-полимеразы длиной более 150 нуклеотидов, ни один из которых не мог реплицировать сам себя. Британские ученые сделали важный шаг к преодолению этих трудностей.

title: Progress in reducing child deaths slows as 4.9 million children die before age five
summary: An estimated 4.9 million children died before their fifth birthday in 2024, including 2.3 million newborns, according to new estimates released today. Most of these deaths are preventable with proven, low-cost interventions and access to quality health care. According to the report – Levels & Trends in Child Mortality – under-five deaths globally have fallen by more than half since 2000. However, since 2015, the pace of reduction in child mortality has slowed by more…

ru.summary: По новым оценкам, опубликованным в настоящий момент, в 2024 году умерло примерно 4,9 миллиона детей в возрасте до пяти лет, включая 2,3 миллиона новорожденных. Большинство этих смертей можно предотвратить с помощью доказанных недорогих мероприятий и качественной медицинской помощи. Согласно отчету «Уровни и тенденции детской смертности», глобальная смертность детей в возрасте до пяти лет снизилась более чем вдвое с 2000 года. Однако с 2015 года темпы снижения детской смертности замедлились.

title: Closing your eyes to hear better might be a big mistake
summary: Many people believe closing their eyes sharpens hearing, but that is not always true. In noisy settings, participants struggled more to hear faint sounds with their eyes closed, while matching visuals made it easier. Researchers found that shutting the eyes leads the brain to over-filter incoming sounds. Keeping your eyes open may actually improve how well you hear in noise.

title: Belly fat linked to heart failure risk even in people with normal weight
summary: Carrying extra fat around the waist may be more dangerous than the number on the scale suggests. Researchers found that belly fat was more strongly linked to heart failure risk than BMI, even in people with normal weight. Inflammation seems to play a key role, helping explain why this type of fat is especially harmful. Measuring waist size could offer a simple way to detect hidden risk earlier.

ru.title: Жир на животе связан с риском сердечной недостаточности даже у людей с нормальным весом

title: The best strength training plan might be simpler than you think
summary: New strength training guidelines emphasize that doing any resistance training is what truly matters. Based on decades of research, experts say even simple routines can increase muscle, strength, and physical function. The key is not perfection but consistency. In fact, the best workout plan is the one you can maintain long term.

ru.title: Лучший план силовых тренировок может быть проще, чем вы думаете

title: What happens after Ozempic shocked researchers
summary: Stopping popular weight-loss injections like Ozempic or Mounjaro might not trigger the dramatic rebound many fear. A large real-world study of nearly 8,000 patients found that most people who discontinue these drugs manage to keep the weight off—or even continue losing—by restarting treatment, switching medications, or adopting lifestyle changes. While earlier clinical trials suggested rapid weight regain, this new evidence paints a more hopeful picture.

ru.title: Что произошло после того, как Озempic поразил исследователей

title: Scientists thought ravens followed wolves. They were wrong
summary: Ravens have long been thought to follow wolves to find food, but new research shows they’re far more strategic. By tracking both animals in Yellowstone, scientists discovered that ravens memorize areas where wolf kills are likely and fly directly to those spots—sometimes from great distances. Rather than trailing wolves, they rely on learned patterns in the landscape. It’s a clever system that highlights just how intelligent these birds really are.

ru.title: Ученые думали, что вороны следуют за волками. Они ошибались

title: Wildfires in carbon-rich tropical peatlands hit 2000-year high
summary: Tropical peatlands, some of the planet’s largest underground carbon stores, are now burning at levels never seen in at least 2,000 years. By analyzing charcoal preserved in peat across multiple continents, scientists discovered that fires had actually been declining for more than a thousand years, largely shaped by natural climate patterns like drought. That long trend suddenly reversed in the 20th century, with a sharp surge in wildfires—especially in Southeast Asia and Australasia.

ru.title: Лесные пожары в богатых углеродом тропических торфяниках достигли максимума за 2000 лет

title: Physicists discover a heavy cousin of the proton at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider
summary: A new subatomic particle known as the Ξcc⁺ has been discovered at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider. This heavy proton-like particle contains two charm quarks and was detected using the upgraded LHCb experiment. Scientists observed it through its decay into lighter particles in high-energy collisions. The finding confirms predictions and settles a decades-long question about its existence.

ru.title: Физики открыли тяжелого кузена протона на Большом адронном коллайдере CERN

title: Scientists recreated a dinosaur nest to solve a 70-million-year-old mystery
summary: Scientists recreated a life-size oviraptor nest to understand how these dinosaurs hatched their eggs. Their experiments showed the parent likely couldn’t heat all the eggs directly, meaning sunlight played a key role. This uneven heating could cause eggs in the same nest to hatch at different times. The results suggest oviraptors used a hybrid incubation method unlike modern birds.

title: You don’t need to lose weight to reverse prediabetes, study finds
summary: For years, people with prediabetes have been told the same thing: lose weight or risk developing diabetes. But new research flips that idea on its head, showing that blood sugar can return to normal even without shedding pounds. The key isn’t just how much fat you carry—it’s where it’s stored. Harmful fat deep in the abdomen fuels inflammation and disrupts insulin, while fat under the skin can actually support healthier metabolism.

ru.title: Исследование показало, что не нужно худеть, чтобы обратить вспять предиабет

title: These strange pink rocks just revealed a hidden giant beneath Antarctica
summary: Pink granite boulders sitting mysteriously atop Antarctica’s Hudson Mountains have led scientists to a stunning discovery: a hidden granite mass buried beneath Pine Island Glacier, stretching nearly 100 km wide and 7 km thick. By dating the rocks to the Jurassic period and matching them with gravity signals detected from aircraft, researchers solved a decades-old puzzle about their origin.

title: Cutting sweet foods doesn’t reduce cravings or improve health
summary: A new study suggests that eating less sweet food doesn’t make people crave it any less—or improve their health markers. Participants who increased or decreased sweetness in their diets showed no changes in preferences, weight, or disease risk. Many even returned to their old eating habits over time. Researchers say it may be time to rethink guidelines that focus on cutting sweetness instead of reducing sugar and calories.

title: The surprising cancer link between cats and humans
summary: Scientists have mapped the genetics of cancer in cats for the first time at scale, uncovering major overlaps with human cancers. Key mutations—like those linked to breast cancer—appear in both species, and some human cancer drugs may also work in cats. Because pets share our environments, these similarities could reveal shared causes of cancer. The research could lead to new treatments that benefit both animals and humans.

ru.title: Удивительная связь между раком у кошек и людей

title: Your daily coffee may be protecting your brain, 43-year study finds
summary: Your morning coffee or tea could be quietly supporting your brain health. A long-term study found that moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee or tea was linked to an 18% lower risk of dementia and better cognitive performance over time. The benefits appeared strongest at 2–3 cups of coffee or 1–2 cups of tea daily—and even held true for people genetically predisposed to dementia.

ru.title: Ваш ежедневный кофе может защищать ваш мозг, показало исследование в течение 43 лет

title: New drug protects liver after intestinal surgery and boosts nutrient absorption
summary: A risky but often lifesaving surgery that removes damaged parts of the small intestine can leave patients facing a new threat: serious liver damage with no available treatment. Now, scientists have developed a promising compound that works directly in the gut to shield the liver and improve how the body absorbs nutrients. In mouse studies, the drug boosted weight gain, reduced harmful liver scarring, and avoided side effects by staying confined to the intestines.

ru.title: Новый препарат защищает печень после кишечной хирургии и улучшает усвоение питательных веществ

title: AI uses as much energy as Iceland but scientists aren’t worried
summary: AI’s growing energy use sounds alarming, but its global climate impact may be far smaller than expected. Researchers found that while AI consumes huge amounts of electricity, it barely moves the needle on overall emissions. The real impact is more localized, especially around data centers. Meanwhile, AI could become a powerful tool for building greener technologies.

ru.title: Искусственный интеллект потребляет столько же энергии, сколько Исландия, но учёные не беспокоятся

title: This simple habit could help seniors live longer and stay independent
summary: Cycling might be one of the simplest ways for older adults to stay healthier, longer. A 10-year study in Japan found that seniors who rode bicycles had lower risks of needing long-term care and dying—especially those who didn’t drive. Continuing or even starting cycling later in life still delivered noticeable benefits. The results highlight biking as a surprisingly powerful tool for maintaining independence and well-being.

ru.title: Эта простая привычка может помочь пожилым людям жить дольше и оставаться независимыми

title: JWST reveals a strange sulfur world unlike any planet we know
summary: Astronomers have identified a strange new kind of exoplanet that challenges how scientists classify worlds beyond our Solar System. The planet, L 98-59 d, appears to contain a vast ocean of molten rock beneath its surface that traps large amounts of sulfur deep inside. Observations from the James Webb Space Telescope revealed unusual sulfur-rich gases in its atmosphere and a surprisingly low density for its size.

ru.title: JWST раскрывает странный серный мир, непохожий на известные нам планеты

title: Scientists used 7,000 GPUs to simulate a tiny quantum chip in extreme detail
summary: Researchers have pushed quantum chip design into a new era by simulating every physical detail before fabrication. Using a supercomputer with nearly 7,000 GPUs, they modeled how signals travel and interact inside an ultra-tiny chip. Unlike earlier “black box” approaches, this method captures real materials, layouts, and qubit behavior. The result is a powerful new way to spot problems early and build better quantum hardware faster.

ru.title: Учёные использовали 7000 графических процессоров для детального моделирования крошечного квантового чипа

title: Scientists finally reveal how this Alzheimer’s drug really works
summary: A key Alzheimer’s drug has finally revealed its secret. Researchers discovered that lecanemab works by activating the brain’s immune cells—but only through a specific part of the antibody called the Fc fragment. This piece acts like a trigger, prompting microglia to clear harmful amyloid plaques. The finding could reshape how future Alzheimer’s therapies are designed.

ru.title: Учёные наконец раскрыли, как действительно работает это лекарство от болезни Альцгеймера

title: Study finds ChatGPT gets science wrong more often than you think
summary: A new study put ChatGPT to the test by asking it to judge whether hundreds of scientific hypotheses were true or false—and the results were far from reassuring. While the AI got it right about 80% of the time on the surface, its performance dropped significantly when accounting for random guessing, revealing only modest reasoning ability. Even more concerning, it frequently contradicted itself when asked the exact same question multiple times, sometimes flipping answers back and forth.

title: Scientists link childhood stress to lifelong digestive issues
summary: Early life stress may set the stage for long-term digestive problems by disrupting the gut-brain connection. Studies in both mice and thousands of children found links to symptoms like pain, constipation, and IBS. Scientists discovered that different biological pathways control different gut issues, hinting at more personalized treatments in the future. The research also highlights how a child’s early environment can have lasting physical effects—not just emotional ones.

title: NASA’s Webb captures a bizarre brain-shaped nebula around a dying star
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has revealed new details in a bizarre nebula that looks like a brain floating in space. Formed by a dying star, the “Exposed Cranium” nebula shows layered gas and a dark central divide that creates its eerie shape. Webb’s infrared view suggests powerful jets may be shaping the structure. The images capture a brief and dramatic phase in a star’s final evolution.

title: A strange twist in the universe’s oldest light may be bigger than we thought

title: Ученый рассказал о двух метеорных потоках в конце марта
summary: В марте 2026 года жители Земли смогут наблюдать два весьма любопытных метеорных потока — Гамма-Нормиды и Эта-Виргиниды. Первый можно будет увидеть вплоть до 28 марта, а активность второго продлится до 25 марта. Из-за отсутствия лунного света небо будет темным, поэтому оба «звездопада» будут хорошо видны. Эксперт объясняет, чем уникален каждый из них, какова их природа, где географически они видны и какие условия нужны, чтобы не пропустить редкие космические вспышки.

title: These dinosaurs had wings but couldn’t fly
summary: Some feathered dinosaurs may have briefly taken to the skies—only to give it up later. By studying rare fossils with preserved feathers, researchers uncovered a surprising clue hidden in molting patterns, revealing that Anchiornis likely couldn’t fly at all. Instead of the neat, symmetrical feather replacement seen in flying birds, these dinosaurs showed a messy, irregular molt—something only flightless animals exhibit.

ru.title: У этих динозавров были крылья, но они не могли летать

title: He survived 48 hours without lungs and lived
summary: A critically ill 33-year-old man survived an almost unimaginable scenario—living for 48 hours without lungs—thanks to a groundbreaking surgical approach. After a severe flu-triggered infection destroyed his lungs and caused multiple organ failure, doctors removed both lungs entirely to stop the spread of infection. In their place, they used a specially engineered “artificial lung” system to oxygenate his blood and keep his body functioning while he stabilized.

ru.title: Он выжил 48 часов без легких и остался в живых

title: Even JWST can’t see through this planet’s massive haze
summary: Kepler-51d is a giant, ultra-light “super-puff” planet wrapped in an unusually thick haze that’s blocking scientists from seeing what it’s made of. Observations from JWST revealed that this haze may be one of the largest ever detected, possibly stretching as wide as Earth itself. The planet’s low density and close orbit don’t match existing models of how gas giants form or survive. Now, researchers are left with more questions than answers about how such a strange world came to be.

title: This massive crater could expose the heart of a lost planet
summary: A mysterious metal-rich asteroid called Psyche has been baffling scientists for over two centuries, and its true origin remains one of the biggest unanswered questions in planetary science. Is it the exposed core of a failed planet, or a chaotic mix of rock and metal forged through countless violent collisions? To find out, researchers simulated how a massive crater near Psyche’s north pole formed, revealing that the asteroid’s internal “porosity” — how much empty space it contains — may hold…

title: Scientists just discovered bull sharks have friends
summary: Bull sharks may have a reputation as lone hunters, but new research reveals they actually form social bonds and even have preferred “friends.” After six years of observing 184 sharks in Fiji, scientists discovered these animals don’t just mix randomly—they choose companions, swim together, and even follow one another in coordinated ways.

title: DNA origami vaccines could be the next leap beyond mRNA
summary: mRNA vaccines saved millions of lives during COVID-19 but have limitations like waning immunity and complex production. Scientists are now testing a new platform called DoriVac, which uses folded DNA nanostructures to better control how the immune system responds. In early studies, it produced strong antibody and T cell responses in both mice and human models. Researchers say it could lead to more stable, easier-to-manufacture vaccines for diseases like COVID-19, HIV, and Ebola.

title: ADHD brains show sleep-like activity even while awake
summary: Researchers have identified a surprising brain pattern that may help explain why people with ADHD often struggle to stay focused. Even while awake, their brains can slip into brief episodes of “sleep-like” activity during demanding tasks. These moments are linked to more mistakes, slower reaction times, and lapses in attention.

title: Fixing a tooth infection may improve blood sugar and heart health
summary: A new clinical study suggests that successful root canal treatment may benefit more than just dental health. Patients treated for infected teeth showed improved blood sugar control, healthier cholesterol levels, and reduced inflammation over time. The infection can allow bacteria to enter the bloodstream, potentially affecting metabolism and heart health. Clearing the infection appears to help reverse some of these harmful effects.

title: The smell of Egyptian mummies is revealing 2,000-year-old secrets
summary: The distinctive smell of ancient mummies is helping scientists decode the secrets of Egyptian mummification. By analyzing tiny traces of chemicals in the air around mummy samples, researchers identified dozens of compounds linked to oils, resins, beeswax, and bitumen used during embalming. The chemical clues reveal that mummification grew increasingly complex over thousands of years.

summary: Scientists have developed a powerful new computational method that could accelerate the search for next-generation materials capable of turning sunlight into useful chemical energy. The work focuses on polyheptazine imides, a promising class of carbon nitride materials that absorb visible light and can drive reactions such as hydrogen production, carbon dioxide conversion, and hydrogen peroxide synthesis. title: A strange new quantum state appears when atoms get “frustrated”
summary: Physicists at UC Santa Barbara have uncovered a new way to manipulate unusual magnetic states by exploiting “frustration” inside a crystal’s atomic structure. The team discovered a rare system where two different kinds of frustration—magnetic and electronic bond frustration—coexist and interact. By coupling these competing effects, researchers may be able to control exotic quantum states, potentially unlocking new ways to manipulate entangled spins for future quantum technologies.

title: Just 24 minutes of specially designed music could significantly reduce anxiety
summary: A clinical trial found that listening to specially designed music with auditory beat stimulation can significantly reduce anxiety. Among several listening lengths tested, a 24-minute session delivered the biggest benefits, easing both mental and physical symptoms of anxiety. The results suggest there may be an ideal “dose” of therapeutic music that works quickly without requiring long listening sessions.

title: Scientists discover what really happens during sourdough fermentation
summary: New research shows that sourdough fermentation does more than make bread rise—it transforms wheat fibers in unexpected ways. Scientists found that enzymes already present in wheat, activated by the sourdough’s acidic environment, break down key fibres called arabinoxylans. This process may influence the bread’s texture, digestibility, and flavor. Some microbes even create buttery or subtly sweet notes that give sourdough its distinctive taste.

title: Common pesticide may more than double Parkinson’s disease risk
summary: A new UCLA Health study suggests that long-term exposure to the pesticide chlorpyrifos may dramatically raise the risk of Parkinson’s disease. Researchers found that people living in areas with sustained exposure had more than 2.5 times the likelihood of developing the disorder. Lab experiments reinforced the finding: animals exposed to the chemical developed movement problems, lost dopamine-producing neurons, and showed the same toxic protein buildup seen in Parkinson’s patients.

title: MIT scientists discover gut protein that traps and kills dangerous bacteria
summary: Scientists at MIT have discovered that a little-known protein called intelectin-2 plays a powerful double role in defending the gut. The protein strengthens the mucus layer that lines the gastrointestinal tract while also trapping and disabling harmful bacteria that try to break through. By binding to sugars on both mucus molecules and bacterial surfaces, intelectin-2 forms a protective barrier and can even destroy microbes, including some that resist antibiotics.

title: Cells can sense 10x farther than expected and it may explain cancer spread
summary: Scientists have discovered that cells can sense far beyond the surfaces they touch. While individual cancer cells can probe about 10 microns ahead by tugging on surrounding collagen fibers, clusters of normal epithelial cells can combine forces to detect layers as far as 100 microns away. This long-range “depth sensing” helps cells decide where to migrate. Understanding how it works could reveal new targets to stop cancer from spreading.
